"«. amit-11017/13/2005-l{'ic:ftl((1]. ~ ~\1qlf414; ~ ~- . ~ ~ :- ~-f.rwr m.cm.f.i. ~C!. 5oo(a:r). ~ 2 'fr,1989 sm Slifltllra ~ ~ ~ ~3fflr:r 'fi:ffii:M ~.cm.f.i. ~o s2s (a:r). ~ 8 Wffif, 2012 em '<i:tn~a fm<rr 1J<IT I 3 -- 3 of 7 -- 4 THE GAZE'ITE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PAKr 11-Sa:. 3(i)J MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS NOTIFICATlON New Delhi, the 31st December, 2012 G.S.R. 943{E).- Whereas the draft rules further to amend ·the Central Motor Vehic~es Rules, 1989 were published,· as required under sub- section (1) of section 212 of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (59 of 1988), . . vide notification of the Government of India In the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways number G.S.R 631 (E), dated the 19th August, 2011 In the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, section (3), Sub-section (i); inviting objections and suggestions from all .persons likely to be affected . . ' thereby before the expiry of the· period of forty five d;;.ys from the date on whiCh copies of the Gazette containing the ·s~id notification were made available to the public; And, whereas, copies of the said Gazette notification were made available to the public on the 19th August, 2011; And whereas; the objections and sugg~stions received from the public in respect of the said draft rules have been considered by the Central Government; Now, therefore, in exercise of the powers ·conf~rred by. clause (f) of . : - ... sub-section (1) of section 110 of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (59 of 1988),· the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend · -· th~ Central Motor Vehicles Rules, 1989, namely: - 1. (1) These rules may be called the Central Motor Vehicles (Fourth Amendment) Rules, 2012. (2) They shall come into force with effect from the 1st day of April, 2014. .;:·· .. -- 4 of 7 -- .r.. ['111'1 n-~ 3(i)] 2. In the Central MotorVehiclf:!s Rules, 1989, for rule 118, the foflowing rule shall be substituted, namely::- •us.Speed governor.- (1) All types o_f transport vehicles notified by the Central Government under sub-section (4) of section 41 of the Motor Vehicles Act, 1988 (59 of 1988) save as prov.ided herein, and manufactured with effect from the 1st April,· 2014 shall be equipped or · fitted with a speed governor (speed limiting device or speed limiting function) with"a m~ximum pre-set speed of 80 kilometer per hour"by t'he ·. vehicle manufactuter in the manufacturing stage or at the dealership · stage, conforming to the Standard AIS:018/2001, as amended from time ·· to time: Pr:cvided that the transport yehicles registered prior tQ . the date specified in sub-rule (1) and as may be notified by the · I State G0vernments in their· Official Gazette, if not already. · · fitted with a speed limiting device or speed limiting function,. . shall be equipped, or fitted by the pperator of such transport... . vehicle with ,a speed governor or speed limltl~g function . having maximum pre-set speed of. 80 kilometer per hour, conforming to the Standard AIS: 018/2001, as amended from time to .time: are - . I Provided further that transport category· vehicles "that {i) two wheelers; (ii) three wheelers; ·· {iii) vehicles with at least four wheels used for . the carriage of passengers and their luggage, not exceeding 3500 kilogram gross vehide weight; (iv) vehicles with at least. four wheels used for carrying goods which may also carry persons in addition to the goods subject to the conditions· specified in para 3.2 of 5 . -- 5 of 7 -- 1liE GAZEITE OF INDIA : EXTRAORiil':·lARY {PAKr ll-8EC. 3(i)] AIS 053-2005, as amended from time to time, till the cortesponding BIS specifications are notified under the Bureau of Indian Standards Act, 1986 (63 of 1986} not exceeding 3500 kg gross vehicle weight; (v) fire tenders; (vi) ambulances; (vii) police vehicles; (viii} vehicles operating on State permit or National . .. . ,Goods Permit o,· r\lllndia Tourist Permit; and . (xi} those transpor.: vehicles whose maximum rated speed does not e}(ceed the pre-set speed of 80 kilometer per hour, need not requir~ Jnstallation of such speed governor (speed lin:"~iting device or speed limiting function). (2) The speed limiting device on all applicable vehicles shall be fitted by the manufacturer or the dealer or the operator, as the case may be, in such a manner that the said device can be sealed with an official seal of the State Transport Authority or a· Regional Transport Authority so that it cannot be removed or tampered with without breaking the seal: Provided that the transport vehicles which are eq,.:lp()~d or fitted with a speed limiting functio11, as specified in clause 3.4 of AIS 018/2001, which controls the speed of vehicles by an electronic control unit, shall not require to be sealed. . . . ~ (3) The speed governor (speed limiting device. or speed limiting function) · shall be so· set that the vehicle is incapable of being driven at a speed in excess of the maximum pre-set speed of the vehicle, except- (i} down an incline; or -- 6 of 7 -- I -- ---, ['IIFI n-~ J(i)J - (il) as specified in clause 5. 7.3.4.2 of AIS 018/2001. Explanation.- For the purpose of this rule, it is clarified that on and from the date of commencement of the Central Motor Vehicles (Fourth Amendment) Rules, 2012, this rule shall prevail over all notifications issued · by the State Governments in respect of spe~d governors." 7 [F. No. RT-11017/13/2005-MVL] SANJAY BANDOPADHYAYA, Jt Secy. Foot Note : The principal rules were notified in the Gazette oflndia vide G.S. R. 590{E} dated 2nd June, 1989 and last amended vide GS.R. 625(E) dated the 8th Aug us~ 2012. . ..
